skill.name: video-prompting-guide
skill.description: Best practices and techniques for writing effective AI video generation prompts. Covers: Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok, Kling, Runway, Pika, Sora prompting strategies. Learn: shot types, camera movements, lighting, pacing, style keywords, negative prompts. Use for: improving video quality, getting consistent results, professional video prompts. Triggers: video prompt, how to prompt video, veo prompts, video generation tips, better ai video, video prompt engineering, video prompt guide, video prompt template, ai video tips, video prompt best practices, video prompt examples, cinematography prompts
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide for writing effective AI video generation prompts across multiple platforms including Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Runway.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation and educational guide for AI video prompting. All 43 static scanner detections are false positives. The detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, cryptographic algorithms) are actually legitimate documentation elements: example CLI commands, reference URLs to the inference.sh service, and markdown table formatting. No actual security risks present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'shell backtick execution' at multiple lines. These are example CLI commands in markdown code blocks (e.g., 'infsh app run google/veo-3-1-fast'), not executable code. This is documentation showing tool usage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Example commands in documentation are clearly marked with bash code fences, not actual shell execution vulnerabilities

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Network URL References
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh service website, not suspicious network calls or data exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official inference.sh service domains for documentation and CLI downloads

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Misclassified Keywords
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. The flagged lines contain documentation tables with keywords like 'tracking shot', 'pan', 'tilt' - standard cinematography terms, not cryptographic code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Scanner misinterprets cinematography terminology as cryptographic terms; no cryptographic algorithms present

content.value_statement: This skill teaches best practices for writing effective AI video generation prompts across Veo, Seedance, Wan, Runway, and other platforms. Learn shot types, camera movements, lighting techniques, and prompt formulas to create professional-quality AI videos.
actual_capabilities: Provide structured formulas for writing video prompts (shot type + subject + action + setting + lighting + style + technical)
Recommend shot types (wide, medium, close-up, aerial, POV) for different video goals
Suggest camera movements (tracking, dolly, pan, tilt, crane, steadicam) for cinematic effects
List lighting keywords (golden hour, blue hour, high key, low key, neon) for mood creation
Offer style keywords (cinematic, minimalist, cyberpunk, noir) for aesthetic consistency
Provide model-specific tips for Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok video generation platforms
limitations: Does not generate videos directly; provides prompting guidance only
Results depend on the underlying AI video model capabilities
Some models may not support all mentioned features (audio, frame interpolation)
Prompt effectiveness varies by model version and updates

use_cases.title: Content Creator Learning Video AI
use_cases.description: A content creator new to AI video generation learns how to write effective prompts for platforms like Veo and Runway to create social media content.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, social media managers, YouTubers

use_cases.title: Video Producer Seeking Consistency
use_cases.description: A video producer uses the prompt structure formula and style keywords to achieve consistent visual quality across multiple AI-generated videos for a brand campaign.
use_cases.target_user: Video producers, marketing teams, brand managers

use_cases.title: Developer Building Video Apps
use_cases.description: A developer integrating AI video generation into an application uses the CLI examples and model-specific tips to guide users on prompt best practices.
use_cases.target_user: Developers, app builders, AI product teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a simple video prompt for [subject] in [setting]. Include what happens in the scene.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user needing a starting point for video generation

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Video Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [shot type] of [subject] [action] in [setting]. Use [lighting] lighting and [style] style. Technical: [resolution], [camera effect].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user wanting more professional, cinematic results

prompt_templates.title: Product Demo Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a smooth [camera movement] shot around [product] on [surface]. Include [lighting], [style], [technical specs]. Show [specific detail].
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating product showcase or advertisement content

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Refinement Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Take this base prompt: '[base prompt]'. Now add specificity for [specific element]. Then add style and technical details including lighting, camera movement, and quality specs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user following iterative prompting workflow

output_examples.input: Write a video prompt for a car commercial
output_examples.output: Cinematic tracking shot of a red sports car driving through Tokyo at night, neon lights reflecting on wet streets, rain falling, 4K, shallow depth of field, film grain, cinematic color grading

output_examples.input: How should I prompt for a product video?
output_examples.output: Smooth tracking shot around a sleek smartphone on a white pedestal, soft studio lighting, product photography style, reflections on surface, 4K, shallow depth of field

best_practices: Use the prompt structure formula: [Shot Type] + [Subject] + [Action] + [Setting] + [Lighting] + [Style] + [Technical]
Start with simple prompts and iterate by adding specificity, then style and technical details
Include camera movements to add dynamic motion; avoid static descriptions without action
anti_patterns: Being too vague (e.g., 'make a nice video') - specify every element clearly
Including conflicting styles (e.g., 'minimalist maximalist') - choose one aesthetic
Creating complex multi-scene prompts - focus on one scene per prompt for best results

faq.question: What AI video platforms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill covers Veo (Google), Seedance (ByteDance), Wan 2.5, Grok, Kling, Runway, Pika, and Sora prompting strategies.

faq.question: Do I need special tools to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. For running video generation, you may use the inference.sh CLI (infsh) as shown in the examples.

faq.question: What is the prompt structure formula?
faq.answer: The formula is: [Shot Type] + [Subject] + [Action] + [Setting] + [Lighting] + [Style] + [Technical]. This ensures comprehensive prompts with all necessary elements.

faq.question: Why are my AI videos coming out blurry?
faq.answer: Add quality keywords like '4K', '8K', 'high resolution', 'photorealistic', and 'shallow depth of field' to your prompts for sharper results.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple camera movements in one prompt?
faq.answer: It is better to focus on one primary camera movement per prompt. Multiple movements may confuse the model and produce unexpected results.

faq.question: How do I get consistent character motion?
faq.answer: Use Seedance for consistent character motion and dance videos. Include reference images and specify the exact actions you want the character to perform.
